steps.title: Spot the key phrase
steps.content: The key phrase: maintain a rigid distinction while avoiding ______ them (the actor and character). The scholars don't want these two to be treated as the same, so the blank must be a word meaning to merge or blur them.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank needs a word meaning to merge or blur two distinct things into one. Substitute each choice:
  • Conflate (A) — to treat two separate things as if they are the same thing. "Taking care never to conflate them" = never treating the actor and character as one entity. ✓
  • Epitomize (B) — to be the perfect example of something. "Never to epitomize them" makes no sense in context.
  • Lambaste (C) — to criticize harshly. Unrelated to merging distinct entities.
  • Overshadow (D) — to be more prominent than something else. Describes a dominance relationship, not a merging.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
